intended_use: Kosmos is intended to be used by qualified and trained healthcare professionals in the clinical assessment for the following clinical applications by acquiring, processing, displaying, measuring, and
intended_use_full: Kosmos is intended to be used by qualified and trained healthcare professionals in the clinical assessment for the following clinical applications by acquiring, processing, displaying, measuring, and storing ultrasound images, or synchronized ultrasound images, electrocardiogram (ECG) rhythms, and digital auscultation (DA) sounds and waveforms. With respect to its ultrasound imaging capabilities, Kosmos is a general purpose diagnostic ultrasound system used in the following clinical applications and modes of operation: Clinical Applications: Cardiac, Thoracic/Lung, Abdominal, Vascular/Peripheral Vascular, Musculoskeletal, and interventional guidance (includes needle/catheter placement, fluid drainage, and nerve block); Modes of Operation: B-mode, M-mode, Color Doppler, Pulsed-Wave (PW) Doppler, Continuous-Wave (CW) Doppler, Combined Modes of B+M, and B+CD, B+PW, B+CW, and Harmonic Imaging. Kosmos is intended to be used in clinical care and medical education settings on adult and pediatric patient populations.

safety_events.narrative_text: ECHONOUS NOTIFIED FDA OF A VOLUNTARY RECALL ON (B)(6) 2024, WITHIN 10 WORKING DAYS FROM AWARENESS. FDA INITIATED RECALL RES 94709 ON (B)(6), 2024. TO DATE, ECHONOUS HAS RECEIVED NO COMPLAINTS RELATED TO THIS ISSUE. GIVEN THERE WAS POTENTIAL FOR SERIOUS INJURY, THIS MDR IS BEING SUBMITTED.

recalls.reason: Ultrasound system has a bug in affected iOS software, which after DICOM export, can cause inaccuracy in postprocessed measurements done in 3rd party software, such as PACS or other post-processing software, which may lead to inaccurate diagnoses or treatment.
recalls.product_description: Kosmos on iOS impacting iOS, Model Numbers: P007639, P007944, software is part of a general-purpose diagnostic ultrasound system.
